question: What are the main approaches for few-shot learning in computer vision and how do they compare in terms of accuracy on benchmark datasets?

gold_papers.title: Matching Networks for One Shot Learning
gold_papers.reason: Seminal work introducing matching networks for few-shot learning with strong empirical results

gold_papers.title: Prototypical Networks for Few-shot Learning
gold_papers.reason: Introduces prototypical networks which learn a metric space for classification

gold_papers.title: Model-Agnostic Meta-Learning for Fast Adaptation of Deep Networks
gold_papers.reason: MAML provides a general meta-learning framework applicable to few-shot learning

gold_papers.title: A Closer Look at Few-shot Classification
gold_papers.reason: Comprehensive empirical study comparing multiple few-shot learning methods

gold_papers.title: Rethinking Few-Shot Image Classification: a Good Embedding Is All You Need?
gold_papers.reason: Challenges existing paradigms and shows strong baselines can be competitive

gold_report_points.point: Matching Networks achieve 46.6% accuracy on 5-way 1-shot ImageNet classification
Prototypical Networks outperform Matching Networks on miniImageNet by 2-3% in both 1-shot and 5-shot settings
MAML achieves fast adaptation within 5-10 gradient steps on new tasks
Simple baseline methods with better feature embeddings can match or exceed complex meta-learning approaches
Few-shot learning methods are typically evaluated on miniImageNet, tieredImageNet, and Omniglot benchmarks

gold_claims.claim: Matching Networks achieve 46.6% accuracy on 5-way 1-shot ImageNet
gold_claims.evidence_snippet: On ImageNet, our approach achieves 46.6% accuracy on one-shot, five-way classification

gold_claims.claim: Prototypical Networks use euclidean distance in embedding space for classification
gold_claims.evidence_snippet: We compute distances to prototype representations of each class and produce a distribution over classes

gold_claims.claim: MAML is model-agnostic and can be applied to any model trained with gradient descent
gold_claims.evidence_snippet: Our algorithm is model-agnostic, in the sense that it can be directly applied to any learning problem and model

gold_claims.claim: The gap between simple baselines and meta-learning methods is smaller than previously reported
gold_claims.evidence_snippet: We show that a simple baseline method can achieve competitive performance with recent meta-learning algorithms

gold_claims.claim: Good feature embeddings are more important than meta-learning algorithms for few-shot classification
gold_claims.evidence_snippet: A good embedding model can significantly boost few-shot classification accuracy, sometimes even surpassing state-of-the-art meta-learning methods

question: How do transformer-based language models handle long-context inputs and what are the computational trade-offs?

gold_papers.title: Attention is All You Need
gold_papers.reason: Original transformer paper establishing the foundation for attention mechanisms

gold_papers.title: Longformer: The Long-Document Transformer
gold_papers.reason: Introduces sparse attention patterns to handle documents up to 4096 tokens efficiently

gold_papers.title: Reformer: The Efficient Transformer
gold_papers.reason: Uses locality-sensitive hashing to reduce attention complexity from O(n^2) to O(n log n)

gold_papers.title: Big Bird: Transformers for Longer Sequences
gold_papers.reason: Combines random, window, and global attention to handle sequences up to 8x longer

gold_papers.title: Efficient Transformers: A Survey
gold_papers.reason: Comprehensive survey categorizing efficient transformer variants by their attention mechanisms

gold_report_points.point: Standard transformer attention has O(n^2) time and memory complexity with respect to sequence length
Longformer uses sliding window attention combined with global attention to achieve O(n) complexity
Reformer achieves memory efficiency through reversible layers and LSH attention, reducing memory from O(n^2) to O(n log n)
Sparse attention patterns can maintain model quality while significantly reducing computational cost
There is a fundamental trade-off between attention pattern expressiveness and computational efficiency in long-context models

gold_claims.claim: Standard transformer attention complexity is O(n^2) where n is sequence length
gold_claims.evidence_snippet: The attention mechanism has a time and space complexity of O(n^2d) where n is the sequence length

gold_claims.claim: Longformer can process sequences up to 4096 tokens on modern GPUs
gold_claims.evidence_snippet: Longformer can handle sequences of length 4,096 on current hardware

gold_claims.claim: Reformer reduces memory consumption to O(n log n) through LSH attention
gold_claims.evidence_snippet: By using locality-sensitive hashing, we reduce the complexity of attention from O(n^2) to O(n log n)

gold_claims.claim: Big Bird achieves 8x longer context than BERT-style models while maintaining accuracy on question answering tasks
gold_claims.evidence_snippet: BigBird can handle sequences 8x longer than BERT, achieving 87.1% F1 on Natural Questions

gold_claims.claim: Efficient transformers can be categorized into fixed patterns, learnable patterns, and low-rank methods
gold_claims.evidence_snippet: We categorize efficient transformers into: fixed attention patterns, learnable patterns, kernelized attention, and low-rank approximations

question: What techniques are used to improve the factual accuracy of large language models and reduce hallucination?

gold_papers.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Knowledge-Intensive NLP Tasks
gold_papers.reason: Introduces RAG which grounds generation in retrieved documents to improve factuality

gold_papers.title: Factuality Enhanced Language Models for Open-Ended Text Generation
gold_papers.reason: Proposes factuality-aware decoding strategies to reduce hallucination during generation

gold_papers.title: Survey of Hallucination in Natural Language Generation
gold_papers.reason: Comprehensive survey defining hallucination types and mitigation strategies

gold_papers.title: Self-Consistency Improves Chain of Thought Reasoning in Language Models
gold_papers.reason: Self-consistency sampling helps filter hallucinated reasoning paths

gold_papers.title: RARR: Researching and Revising What Language Models Say, Using Language Models
gold_papers.reason: Introduces iterative research-revise framework to fact-check and correct LLM outputs

gold_report_points.point: RAG improves factuality by retrieving relevant documents before generation, achieving 44.5% accuracy on Natural Questions
Hallucinations in LLMs can be categorized into intrinsic (contradicting source) and extrinsic (unverifiable claims)
Self-consistency sampling generates multiple reasoning paths and selects the most consistent answer, improving accuracy by 17% on math reasoning
Post-hoc verification and revision using external knowledge can reduce factual errors by 30-50%
Current factuality improvements often trade off between accuracy gains and increased computational cost

gold_claims.claim: RAG achieves 44.5% exact match on Natural Questions open-domain QA
gold_claims.evidence_snippet: RAG achieves 44.5% exact match accuracy on Natural Questions, outperforming the DPR baseline

gold_claims.claim: Intrinsic hallucination occurs when generated text contradicts the source, while extrinsic hallucination cannot be verified from the source
gold_claims.evidence_snippet: We distinguish intrinsic hallucination (output contradicts source) from extrinsic hallucination (output cannot be verified)

gold_claims.claim: Self-consistency improves accuracy by 17.9% on GSM8K math problems compared to greedy decoding
gold_claims.evidence_snippet: Self-consistency boosts accuracy from 57.1% to 74.4% on GSM8K, an improvement of 17.3 percentage points

gold_claims.claim: RARR reduces factual errors by 30% through iterative research and revision
gold_claims.evidence_snippet: RARR reduces factual error rate by 30% on average across multiple benchmarks

gold_claims.claim: Retrieval latency in RAG systems adds 50-200ms per query depending on corpus size
gold_claims.evidence_snippet: Retrieval adds approximately 100ms latency for our dense passage retrieval over Wikipedia
